title: Debt
description: Practice understanding of debt, factors affecting debt, good debts vs bad debt

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following options would be considered *good* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: borrowing money to go on a vacation
using a credit card to buy clothes you don't need
taking out a loan to pay for job training

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following options would be considered *good* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: taking out a loan to replace your furniture
using a credit card to pay for dinner every night
borrowing money to attend a professional development conference

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following options would be considered *good* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: borrowing money from your boss to attend a concert
using a credit card to pay for your wedding
taking out a loan to buy a house

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of *bad* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: borrowing money to start your own business
taking out a loan to pay for home improvements
using a loan to buy a faster gaming laptop

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of *bad* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: securing a loan for purchasing a property
taking out a loan to expand a business
using a credit card to go on a vacation

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of *bad* debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: applying for a loan to buy a rental property
taking out a loan to fund a promising startup idea
borrowing money for a big wedding

assessment_items.question_content:
Debt can be a helpful tool when used responsibly, but it can also be harmful when not managed properly. Sometimes, good debt can turn into bad debt, and vice versa. 

** When does a student loan, which is considered good debt, turn into bad debt?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when you drop out of college
when you graduate from college
when you get a job

assessment_items.question_content:
Debt can be a helpful tool when used responsibly, but it can also be harmful when not managed properly. Sometimes, good debt can turn into bad debt, and vice versa. 

** When does a mortgage, commonly viewed as good debt, become bad debt?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: when your home's value decreases significantly
when you renovate your home
when you pay off the mortgage early

assessment_items.question_content:
Debt can be a helpful tool when used responsibly, but it can also be harmful when not managed properly. Sometimes, good debt can turn into bad debt, and vice versa. 

** Which of the following is an example of bad debt turning into good debt?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: using a personal loan to pay off high-interest credit card debt
taking out a loan to start a business that fails
borrowing money to buy a new smartphone

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important to recognize the warning signs of getting into debt trouble. By identifying these signs early on, you can take steps to avoid or manage your debt.

**What is one common warning sign of being in debt trouble?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: not having money on you because it is all in savings accounts
only using cash for purchases
making minimum payments on your credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important to recognize the warning signs of getting into debt trouble. By identifying these signs early on, you can take steps to avoid or manage your debt.

**Which of these behaviors might indicate that you're in debt trouble?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: saving a big portion of your income
cutting back on unnecessary expenses
occasionally borrowing money from friends or family to pay your bills

assessment_items.question_content:
It is important to recognize the warning signs of getting into debt trouble. By identifying these signs early on, you can take steps to avoid or manage your debt.

**What is a warning sign of being in debt trouble?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: planning for big future expenses
cutting back on unnecessary expenses
receiving calls from debt collectors
